document.title: Software Validation (Firmware) For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for HAD Firmware used to control X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents firmware software description, requirements, architecture, development procedure, review results, dynamic tests, unresolved bugs, and risk analysis.
keyPoints: HAD Firmware controls the detector, digitizes and transmits analog image data, processes image transformation register values, and communicates with a PC via TCP/IP.
Operational environment is ANSI C on i.MX6S MCIMX6S5DVM10AC with Linux.
The software architecture consists of Initialize, Main, Image data control, and Communication control modules.
Code review, module review, integration review, and dynamic testing are reported as passed or OK.
The report states there were no bugs subjected to safety and effectiveness of firmware.

evidence.claim: The document is a software validation report for firmware for X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation (Firmware) For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-009 Rev.0.
The report was initially issued on March/12/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 March/12/2019 Initially issued to Software validation.

evidence.claim: The firmware software name is HAD Firmware.
The role of the software is to control the detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Role of S/W : Control the detector.

evidence.claim: The firmware digitizes and transmits analog images received from ROIC.
evidence.evidenceText: Digitize and transmit analog images of objects received from ROIC

evidence.claim: The firmware communicates with the PC via TCP/IP to send and receive data.
evidence.evidenceText: Communicate with the PC via the TCP/IP interface to send/receive data

evidence.claim: The operational environment uses ANSI C, i.MX6S MCIMX6S5DVM10AC, and Linux.
evidence.evidenceText: Program language : ANSI C Hardware platform : i.MX6S MCIMX6S5DVM10AC Operation system : Linux

evidence.claim: The software architecture consists of four independently functioning modules combined by the Main Module.
evidence.evidenceText: The software architecture consists of 4 modules and each module functions independently. Main Module combines it into a software unit.

evidence.claim: The Initialize module reads initial values from flash memory and sets up FPGA based on initial values.
evidence.evidenceText: Read Initial value from Flash Memory and setup FPGA based on initial value.

evidence.claim: The Communication control module processes detector image acquisition commands and sends digitized images.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector Image Acquisition Command Processing and Send Digitized Images.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows a classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at VIEWORKS. follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: The report states there were no bugs subjected to safety and effectiveness of firmware.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no bugs that are subjected to safety and effectiveness of firmware.

evidence.claim: Software revision 1.0 was initially released on March/12/2019.
Code review concluded that software verification was properly accomplished.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the code review, we hereby certify that the software verification is properly accomplished

evidence.claim: Module review concluded all module tests were completed successfully.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of module review, all the module tests are completed successfully by utilizing communication function evaluated in the code review.

evidence.claim: Integration review concluded all functions supporting system specification were proper.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of Integration review, we hereby certify that all the functions that support system specification are proper.

evidence.claim: Dynamic tests for booting, dark frame acquisition, flat frame acquisition, FOV, control value read/write/write protection, and control value range limit test were OK.
evidence.evidenceText: Booting process OK Dark frame acquisition OK Flat frame acquisition OK FOV OK Control value Read, Write, Write protection test OK Check control value exceeds range OK

evidence.claim: Risk controls include CRC and command validation check for wrong command code.
evidence.evidenceText: Using CRC and command validation check. Distinguish bad command from incoming data.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is explicitly identified in the source text.
